what_it_does: A JSON capability catalog keyed by model ID that records what each model can do using provenanced {value, source, note} fields and a provides tag set; it deliberately stores only intrinsic model abilities and leaves runtime/VRAM data to other layers.
observations: Each model entry carries tools, json_schema, thinking, context_ceiling, concurrency_knee, speed_profile, and provides.
Every field value is wrapped in a provenance object with value, source, and an explanatory note.
Sources used are declared, probed, measured, and served.
Provides tags are drawn from a CAPABILITY_TAGS vocabulary such as chat, json, tools, fast, no-think, thinking, reasoning, embed, rerank, vision, tts, stt.
The file distinguishes served vLLM models, on-disk-not-yet-served models, non-vLLM engines, and cloud Ollama models.
Several on-disk retrieval models are cataloged, including code embedders, late-interaction retrievers, rerankers, and multimodal vision embedders.
Unknown or unverified capabilities are recorded as null with source declared rather than fabricated.
The file is truncated near the glm-5.1:cloud entry.
Some entries include operational notes about co-residency, swap hostility, quantization, and license restrictions.
The audio codec hubertsiuzdak/snac_24khz has an empty provides list because it is not role-bindable.
standouts: The kimi-k2.7-code:cloud entry is explicitly probed and notes a litellm proxy authentication issue, directing use via OLLAMA_DIRECT.
cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit has a measured concurrency_knee with loadout-specific KV pool calculations.
The file catalogs unserved on-disk models such as LateOn-Code, Qwen3-VL embedding/reranker pair, and multiple rerankers.
It records honest gaps, for example json_schema null for many Ollama models with a note that strict grammar decode is unverified.
conventions: All capability values use the {value, source, note} structure.
On-disk-not-yet-served models are flagged in _note with 'ON DISK ... NOT yet served'.
Served vLLM tags are grounded in services.json flags such as --tool-call-parser, chat_template_nothink, and --runner pooling.
Cloud models carry context_ceiling but explicitly note no local VRAM slot.
Null is used for unknown/unverified values rather than false or a guessed number.
concerns: The file is truncated, so the last entry is incomplete.
Long _doc and notes embed many cross-file policy references that could drift.
Some entries declare tools/json capability while simultaneously noting they have not been live-probed on that exact model ID.
The distinction between 'json' in provides and json_schema value null may be confusing without reading the note.
notes: A provenance-heavy JSON registry that declares what each model ID can do, separating intrinsic capabilities from service config and GPU data, with explicit gaps for unverified or cloud-hosted models.
questions: Where is the CAPABILITY_TAGS vocabulary formally defined?
How is the fail-loud unknown-to-ASK path implemented in code?
What schema validation or test enforces the provenance structure?
How are on-disk models promoted to served and their sources updated?
What entries are missing after the truncation point?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it acts as a single-source intrinsic capability catalog with provenance and explicit unknowns; the only mismatch is that the file is truncated.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between capabilities, service config, and VRAM
Grounded capability tagging from deployment signals
intention_signals: The file is trying to stop agents from guessing model abilities by centralizing a grounded, versioned, test-asserted capability ledger that can be safely joined with service and GPU layers.
novelty: The explicit use of provenance objects and honest nulls for unknowns, plus cataloging unserved on-disk retrieval, reranker, and vision models alongside an empty-provides audio codec.
contribution: Provides a structured, auditable capability vocabulary that a fused system can use to match roles to models without hardcoding assumptions, while clearly separating what is known, measured, declared, or still unknown.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON catalog mapping model IDs to intrinsic capability records with provenance, separating served, on-disk, and cloud models and leaving unknowns explicitly null.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source of truth for what each model can do so that downstream planners can join it with service and GPU data.
